66423ff4515f1173a58a27fa.leadin: This country is home to the world’s longest continuously-operating amusement park, the Bakken. For 10 points each:
66423ff4515f1173a58a27fa.leadin_sanitized: This country is home to the world's longest continuously-operating amusement park, the Bakken. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this southernmost Scandinavian country, whose island of Zealand contains its capital of Copenhagen.
Denmark comprises this peninsula and many islands. The southern portion of this peninsula is owned by Germany as part of the state of Schleswig-Holstein (SHLESS-wig HOLE-stine).
This city, Denmark’s second largest and the largest on Jutland (JUT-lund), is located next to the Kattegat (KAT-uh-gat). This city is nicknamed “the world’s smallest big city.”
parts_sanitized: Name this southernmost Scandinavian country, whose island of Zealand contains its capital of Copenhagen.
Denmark comprises this peninsula and many islands. The southern portion of this peninsula is owned by Germany as part of the state of Schleswig-Holstein (SHLESS-wig HOLE-stine).
This city, Denmark's second largest and the largest on Jutland (JUT-lund), is located next to the Kattegat (KAT-uh-gat). This city is nicknamed "the world's smallest big city."
answers: Jutland Peninsula [or Jylland Peninsula; accept Cimbric peninsula or Cimbrian peninsula]
answers_sanitized: Jutland Peninsula [or Jylland Peninsula; accept Cimbric peninsula or Cimbrian peninsula]

66423ff4515f1173a58a2817.question: A type of these materials is predicted by the Hubbard model to form because of the emergence of antiᐧferroᐧmagnetic order; that type of these materials is named for Mott. One type of these materials has surface states protected by time-reversal symmetry; that is their “topological” type. One of these materials is placed between two superᐧconductors in a Josephson junction. These materials have a large (*) bandᐧgap. A polarizable (po-luh-RISE-uh-buhl) type of these materials is placed between the plates of a capacitor (kuh-PA-suh-tur) as dielectrics (dai-uh-LEK-trix). These materials have high resistivity (ruh-zi-STI-vuh-tee). For 10 points, porcelain, rubber, and glass are examples of what materials, which do not conduct electricity?
66423ff4515f1173a58a2817.question_sanitized: A type of these materials is predicted by the Hubbard model to form because of the emergence of antiᐧferroᐧmagnetic order; that type of these materials is named for Mott. One type of these materials has surface states protected by time-reversal symmetry; that is their "topological" type. One of these materials is placed between two superᐧconductors in a Josephson junction. These materials have a large (*) bandᐧgap. A polarizable (po-luh-RISE-uh-buhl) type of these materials is placed between the plates of a capacitor (kuh-PA-suh-tur) as dielectrics (dai-uh-LEK-trix). These materials have high resistivity (ruh-zi-STI-vuh-tee). For 10 points, porcelain, rubber, and glass are examples of what materials, which do not conduct electricity?
66423ff4515f1173a58a2817.answer: insulator [accept Mott insulator or topological insulator; prompt on dielectric before mention]
66423ff4515f1173a58a2817.answer_sanitized: insulator [accept Mott insulator or topological insulator; prompt on dielectric before mention]

66423ff5515f1173a58a285e.leadin: The light-dependent reactions of this process occur in the thylakoid (THAI-luh-koyd) membrane. For 10 points each:
66423ff5515f1173a58a285e.leadin_sanitized: The light-dependent reactions of this process occur in the thylakoid (THAI-luh-koyd) membrane. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this process in which plants use sunlight to synthesize sugars for energy. It mostly occurs in the chloroplasts and requires the green pigment chlorophyll.

These light-independent reactions of photosynthesis begin with RuᐧBisᐧCO reacting carbon dioxide with ribulose-1,5-bisᐧphosphate to form 3-phosphoᐧglycerate. It has fixation, reduction, and regeneration phases.

This metabolic pathway separates carbon fixation reactions temporally. In this pathway, at night, CᐧOᐧ2 is fixed into malate (MA-layt), which is stored in vacuoles (VA-kyoo-ulz), then transformed into sugars during the day.
parts_sanitized: Name this process in which plants use sunlight to synthesize sugars for energy. It mostly occurs in the chloroplasts and requires the green pigment chlorophyll.

These light-independent reactions of photosynthesis begin with RuᐧBisᐧCO reacting carbon dioxide with ribulose-1,5-bisᐧphosphate to form 3-phosphoᐧglycerate. It has fixation, reduction, and regeneration phases.

This metabolic pathway separates carbon fixation reactions temporally. In this pathway, at night, CᐧOᐧ2 is fixed into malate (MA-layt), which is stored in vacuoles (VA-kyoo-ulz), then transformed into sugars during the day.
answers_sanitized: Calvin cycle [accept Calvin-Benson-Bassham cycle; prompt on dark reactions]
CAM pathway [or Crassulacean Acid Metabolism pathway]
